content.title: First Read Rule
body: If this is the agent's first time reading this README for the current work mission, read the entire README before opening deeper docs, following local routes, or relying on this corpus.

A first full pass is required once per current work mission, after a context reset, after a handoff or resume, or after the task materially changes. It is not required at every checkpoint in the same active mission.

After the first full pass, use the routing sections below to decide which local docs to reread or inspect next. At later checkpoints, reread only route-relevant sections unless the active `.core.*` route, roadmap, audit, or local stop condition requires a broader reread.
Do not treat this README as authority over App Behavior, architecture, implementation plans, proof, source, repo facts, product decisions, or explicit user instruction.
Purpose: Index for UX system validation and maintenance tools.
Audience: Agents and engineers checking UX JSON syntax, schema conformance, and corpus hygiene.
Keep In Sync With: UX schemas, examples, model docs, and validation expectations.
Update When: Validation commands, checks, schema locations, or generated artifact tooling changes.
Does Not Cover: Production application runtime validation or generated Markdown rendering.

content.title: Tools
body: This folder contains dependency-free local tools for validating the UX system corpus.
Files are prefixed in recommended reading order.
2. [Wrap Behavior Threads](wrap-behavior-threads.cjs) wraps full-app UX audit rows in parent-container behavior threads with dry-run, fingerprint, row-count, and idempotence guardrails.

content.title: Routing
body: - validating UX system JSON, schema conformance, behavior-thread wrapping, or corpus hygiene
- generated Markdown rendering rules by itself
- wrap-behavior-threads.cjs when wrapping full-app audit rows
- the agent can name the validation command, scope, dry-run/check posture, and whether any writes are authorized
